User Guide

What this tool does

From two inputs — your contact info and a public-domain advocate — it generates a 5-page mailing package: a personalized cover letter (page 1) followed by the 4-page Patent Clause brochure (pages 2–5).

How to use it

1. Enter your name, email, mailing address, and phone. Click Save My Info so you never re-type it.
2. Click Continue. Click the Choose a Public Domain Advocate button. A popup list appears with all 27 advocates; each row wraps to two lines showing name, affiliation, and what that person is known for. Click a row to select.
3. (Optional) Edit the leadership name, title, or address if you have more current information.
4. Click Preview & Print. Use Ctrl+P / Cmd+P or the toolbar button to print or save as PDF.

Brochure images

The cover poster on page 2 and the world map on page 4 are embedded directly in this file. No assets/ folder dependency — the images render in any browser, from any location on disk, with no network access.

Nine-line letter head

Lines 1–3 (sender) are right-aligned, single-spaced. Line 4 (date) is centered. Lines 5–9 (addressee) are left-aligned: leadership name, bio fragment (first 35 characters or up to a semicolon), organization, street, city/state/ZIP.

Editing the cover letter

The cover letter page is editable in preview — click anywhere in the letter to make changes before printing. Brochure pages are not editable.

📋 Wrong Address Report

Review or edit the text below, then copy and paste into an email to roleigh@pobox.com.

Write to a Public Domain Advocate to Restore the Public's Right to Remanufacture Expired Patented Products

The Constitution's Patent Clause promises that when patents expire, inventions pass into the public domain — free to remanufacture. The Supreme Court affirms this.

Since 1970, federal regulations have quietly broken that promise. Products whose patents expired 20+ years ago cannot be remanufactured — at a cost of about $2,420 per American household every year.

The U.S. public-domain scholars, litigators, and public intellectuals in this database have the credentials to bring the constitutional question forward. This tool generates a cover letter and 4-page brochure addressed to any one of them.

Your Information

This information appears on the cover letter as the sender. It’s stored only in your browser.

Covers 27 public-domain advocates across 10 states and DC. Parallel campaigns target county sheriffs, governors, state attorneys general, tribal nations, owner-operator trucking, farm associations, and healthy-lighting advocates.

Selected Public Domain Advocate

Click the button at the bottom to create the full mailing package. Each entry in the popup shows name, affiliation, and what the person is known for. After you pick one, the advocate’s address, biographical summary, and personalized reason-for-callout auto-populate. You may edit any field.
